title: Plagistra cretica
taxonomy: Plagistra cretica is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Gesneriaceae. It is a member of the genus Plagistra and is native to the Mediterranean region. The Gesneriaceae family is a group of flowering plants commonly known as the African violet family. The family consists of around 160 genera and over 3,000 species. Plagistra cretica is one of the lesser-known species within this family.

The most common herbal preparations made with Plagiosperma microphyllum include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
